title: bigelowia pulchella
intro: Bigelowia pulchella, also known as the Bigelow's woolly marigold, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and reducing pain. The health benefits of Bigelowia pulchella include its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema. Herbal preparations of Bigelowia pulchella can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's roots and stems to make a decoction for medicinal purposes.
